mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi Kafka Operator
mission.description: Strimzi Kafka Operator allows you to run Apache Kafka clusters on Kubernetes and OpenShift. It simplifies the deployment and management of Kafka, providing a robust solution for streaming data applications. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Strimzi Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Strimzi Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Strimzi Kafka Operator.

```bash

helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts/

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Strimzi repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator in the 'kafka' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kafka

helm install strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Kafka resources.
> Installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator.

steps.title: Create a Kafka Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Kafka cluster by applying a Custom Resource (CR) definition to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: kafka.strimzi.io/v1beta2
kind: Kafka
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: kafka
spec:
  kafka:
    replicas: 3
    listeners:
      plain:
        port: 9092
        authentication:
          type: tls
    storage:
      type: ephemeral
  zookeeper:
    replicas: 3
    storage:
      type: ephemeral
EOF

```

> Creates a Kafka cluster named 'my-cluster'.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: kafka.strimzi.io/v1beta2
kind: Kafka
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: kafka
spec:
  kafka:
    replicas: 3
    listeners:
      plain:
        port: 9092
        authentication:
          type: tls
    storage:
      type: ephemeral
  zookeeper:
    replicas: 3
    storage:
      type: ephemeral
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Kafka Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks if the Kafka cluster is running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka

kubectl describe kafka my-cluster -n kafka

```

> Lists Kafka clusters in the 'kafka' namespace.
> Describes the Kafka cluster to check its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Strimzi Kafka Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Strimzi Kafka Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Kafka cluster and all associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall strimzi --namespace kafka

kubectl delete namespace kafka

```

> Uninstalls the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Deletes the Kafka namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your Kafka data. This step upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator to a new version.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

helm upgrade strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

kubectl get kafka -n kafka

```

> Backs up the current Kafka configuration.
> Upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Verifies the Kafka cluster is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kafka -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kafka cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka cluster may not have enough resources allocated or may be misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation and configuration in the Kafka Custom Resource.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Zookeeper'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Zookeeper may not be running or is unreachable due to network policies.

**Fix:**
Ensure Zookeeper pods are running and check network policies.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient storage for Kafka'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The storage class may not be available or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the storage class configuration and ensure it is available.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'TLS handshake failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** TLS configuration may be incorrect or certificates may not be valid.

**Fix:**
Check the TLS configuration in the Kafka Custom Resource.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kafka 3.4.0, improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of 'spec.kafka.storage.type: ephemeral' in favor of persistent storage.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.43, ensure to migrate to persistent storage for Kafka.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kafka cluster listed with the status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get kafka -n kafka'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
